examples.title: Basic Shader Program Creation
examples.description: Step-by-step process to create and use a shader program in OpenGL
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the complete workflow of creating a shader program: creating shader objects, loading source code, compiling, checking for errors, attaching to a program, linking, and finally using it for rendering.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for shader compilation where n is shader source length
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for API calls, O(n) for shader storage in GPU memory

examples.title: Vertex Buffer Setup with VAO
examples.description: Modern OpenGL approach to setting up vertex buffers and attributes using Vertex Array Objects
examples.explanation: VAOs encapsulate all VBO bindings and attribute configurations. Once set up, a single glBindVertexArray() call restores the entire state, simplifying draw calls and state management.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of vertices
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for vertex data storage in GPU memory, O(1) for attribute pointers

examples.title: Frame Buffer Object Setup for Off-Screen Rendering
examples.description: Creating an FBO to render scene to a texture instead of the screen
examples.explanation: FBOs allow rendering to textures instead of the screen. This is essential for post-processing effects (bloom, blur), shadow mapping, reflections, and any multi-pass rendering technique.
examples.timeComplexity: O(width × height) for rendering to FBO
examples.spaceComplexity: O(width × height) for color texture + O(width × height) for depth buffer
